facts.fact: Vercel's CDN operates 126+ Points of Presence (PoPs) across 51 countries and 20+ Vercel regions.
Vercel's CDN is framework-aware: it reads routing, caching, and rendering configuration at build time, so caching policies are an output of the build process for supported frameworks (zero config).
Every Vercel deployment includes unmetered, always-on DDoS mitigation and network-level security at no extra cost.

Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR) serves cached pages while regenerating content in the background; Vercel manages caching, request collapsing, and purging automatically when ISR is used with Next.js, SvelteKit, Nuxt, or Astro.

Vercel maintains two distinct caches: the CDN cache (stores responses across regions near visitors) and the runtime cache (stores fetch results, database queries, and computed values inside functions).

Every Vercel deployment uses HTTPS with automatically provisioned SSL certificates and TLS 1.2/1.3 support, plus a platform-wide firewall and optional project-level Web Application Firewall (WAF) with custom rules.
In the Next.js App Router, ISR is enabled by adding a revalidate property (e.g. next: { revalidate: 10 } in seconds) to fetch requests.
On Vercel, framework-aware infrastructure enables global content updates (ISR page propagation) in 300ms, and generated pages are both cached and persisted to durable storage.

On Vercel, Next.js SSR runs through Vercel Functions which scale to zero when idle and scale automatically with traffic, with zero-config support for Cache-Control headers including stale-while-revalidate.

Vercel is positioned as 'the AI Cloud', a unified platform for building, deploying, and scaling AI-powered applications including AI SDK, AI Gateway (provider routing with automatic failover), v0, MCP servers, and Sandbox.
Self-hosted Next.js ISR is limited to a single region and does not persist generated pages to durable storage by default (files live in the expiring Next.js cache).

The CDN evaluates routing rules before checking any cache: redirects return a new URL to the client, rewrites map a public URL to a different backend path (including external rewrites to proxy/cache external backends), and header rules modify request/response metadata.
Next.js streaming on Vercel is supported via Route Handlers, Vercel Functions, and React Server Components, using the loading file convention or React 18 Suspense components for loading states.
Vercel can invalidate cached content by tag (purge) across all frameworks and backends, and offers image optimization to modern formats like WebP and AVIF cached on the CDN.
